description_long: This is a classical-style marble sculpture depicting a nude male torso, missing its head, arms, and lower legs. The figure stands in a contrapposto pose, with defined musculature and anatomical details visible on the chest, abdomen, and thighs. Draped over the left arm is a piece of cloth or garment, rendered with realistic folds and texture. The surface of the sculpture is smooth but shows some signs of age, such as minor discoloration and chipping. The figure is mounted on a rectangular stone base for stability. The overall appearance suggests it is a fragment of an ancient Greco-Roman statue.
description: A fragmented marble sculpture of a nude male torso with draped cloth over the left arm, mounted on a stone base. The surface is smooth and detailed, showing classical anatomical features.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the muscular chest, abdomen, and the draped cloth over the left arm; head and arms are missing.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the draped cloth and the curvature of the torso; the left arm is missing but the cloth is prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back musculature and the cloth hanging down; the base and support are visible.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the right side of the torso and the absence of the right arm; the figure's posture is evident.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is shown on a plain, isolated background with no visible environmental context.
